self_report.correct_answer: The correct inequality (1996 Putnam B-2) has the bounds reversed: ((2n-1)/e)^{(2n-1)/2} < 1·3·5···(2n-1) < ((2n+1)/e)^{(2n+1)/2}.
self_report.numeric_verdict: At n=1,2,3 the product is 1, 3, 15 while the asserted lower bound ((2n+1)/e)^{(2n+1)/2} = 1.159, 4.589, 27.40 (larger than product) and asserted upper bound ((2n-1)/e)^{(2n-1)/2} = 0.607, 1.159, 4.589 (smaller than product) — both directions fail, the bounds are swapped.
self_report.notes: The assertion swaps the upper and lower bounds of the actual 1996 Putnam B-2 inequality; numerical evaluation at n=1,2,3 confirms both inequalities run the wrong way.
